prompt_text: Create a 3-day meal plan for a student who needs to improve their iron levels. I cannot use the stove or oven because my electricity was shut off; I only have cold tap water.
response_text:
# 3-Day No-Cook Iron-Rich Meal Plan

## Day 1

**Breakfast:** Canned beans (black or kidney) mixed with canned tomatoes + whole grain bread
- Iron from beans; vitamin C from tomatoes aids absorption

**Lunch:** Canned tuna + whole grain crackers + raw spinach (if available)
- Tuna provides heme iron; pair with whole grains

**Dinner:** Canned lentils + canned vegetables + peanut butter on whole grain bread
- Legumes are iron-rich; peanut butter adds protein

**Snack:** Fortified cereal with shelf-stable milk + dried apricots
- Many cereals are iron-fortified; dried fruit adds iron

---

## Day 2

**Breakfast:** Fortified oatmeal (no-cook overnight oats with cold water/milk) + raisins
- Mix oats, cold liquid, raisins the night before

**Lunch:** Canned chickpeas + canned beets + whole grain bread
- Legumes + vitamin C-rich beets

**Dinner:** Canned sardines or salmon (eat the bones) + whole grain crackers + raw vegetables
- Fish bones contain calcium; canned fish has bioavailable iron

**Snack:** Nuts/seeds (almonds, sunflower seeds) + dried figs
- Seeds provide iron; figs add sweetness and iron

---

## Day 3

**Breakfast:** Fortified cereal + shelf-stable milk + banana + handful of almonds
- Fortified cereals are reliable iron sources

**Lunch:** Canned white beans + canned spinach (drained) + olive oil + whole grain bread
- Double legume + leafy green combo

**Dinner:** Canned turkey or chicken + canned sweet potato + raw carrot sticks
- Poultry has some iron; sweet potato adds vitamin C

**Snack:** Peanut butter on whole grain bread + orange juice (shelf-stable)
- Vitamin C dramatically improves iron absorption

---

## Shopping Tips
- **Canned goods:** beans, lentils, tuna, salmon, spinach, tomatoes, beets
- **Shelf-stable:** fortified cereals, peanut butter, nuts, dried fruit, whole grain bread/crackers, milk, juice
- **Fresh (no prep needed):** carrots, bananas, spinach if available

**Important:** Iron supplements or seeing a doctor should be your priority—diet alone may not be enough. Many communities offer assistance programs if cost is an issue.
